welcomeMessage: Hello! Welcome to WhiteHouse Education Foundation. I'm here to help you with admissions, programs, scholarships, campus life, and more. How can I assist you today?
fallbackMessage: I couldn't find a specific answer to that. Please contact us at info@whitehouseeducation.edu.np or call 9801268585. You can also reach us at 9801268585.
offTopicMessage:
I'm here to help with questions about WhiteHouse College of Business & Technology (WCBT) only — such as programs, admissions, fees, scholarships, campus facilities, eligibility, and contact details.

Please ask something related to the college or our courses.

entries.question: Hello
entries.answer:
Hello! Welcome to WhiteHouse College of Business & Technology (WCBT). I can help you with programs, admissions, fees, scholarships, campus facilities, and more.

What would you like to know?

entries.question: Thank you
entries.answer: You're welcome! If you have more questions about WCBT, admissions, or our programs, feel free to ask anytime.

entries.question: Goodbye
entries.answer:
Goodbye! Thank you for visiting WCBT. We hope to see you on campus soon.

For admissions help: info@whitehouseeducation.edu.np | 9801268585

entries.question: What can you help me with?
entries.answer:
I can help you with information about:
• WCBT & WEF overview
• Programs (BIT & B.Tech Ed IT)
• Admissions & eligibility
• Fees & scholarships
• Campus facilities & student life
• Careers & internships
• Contact details

Try asking a question or tap a quick question below!

entries.question: Who are you?
entries.answer: I'm WH Assistant, the virtual help desk for WhiteHouse College of Business & Technology (WCBT). I'm here to answer your questions about our college, programs, and admissions.

entries.question: How are you?
entries.answer:
I'm doing great, thank you for asking! I'm ready to help you learn more about WCBT.

Would you like to know about our programs, admissions, or campus?

entries.question: Okay
entries.answer: Glad that helped! Feel free to ask if you need anything else about WCBT — programs, fees, scholarships, or how to apply.

entries.question: Sorry
entries.answer: No worries at all! How can I help you with WCBT today?

entries.question: I want more information
entries.answer:
I'd be happy to share more! You can ask about:
• BIT or B.Tech Ed IT programs
• Admission steps & eligibility
• Fees & scholarships
• Campus facilities in Jhapa

What topic interests you most?

entries.question: Is anyone available to talk?
entries.answer:
For personal assistance, please contact our admissions team:
Phone: 9801268585
Mobile: 9801268585
Email: info@whitehouseeducation.edu.np

Our team will be happy to guide you!

entries.question: What is WhiteHouse College of Business & Technology (WCBT)?
entries.answer:
WhiteHouse College of Business & Technology (WCBT) is a higher education institution established under WhiteHouse Education Foundation (WEF) with a vision to provide quality, technology-driven, industry-oriented education in Eastern Nepal.

The college aims to become a leading center for Information Technology, Innovation, Research, Entrepreneurship, and Digital Transformation.

entries.question: What is WhiteHouse Education Foundation (WEF)?
entries.answer:
WhiteHouse Education Foundation (WEF) is an educational organization dedicated to academic excellence, innovation, research, skill development, and community engagement.

WEF promotes quality education through schools, colleges, research initiatives, professional training, and industry collaboration.

entries.question: Where is WCBT located?
entries.answer:
WCBT operates from the Siddhartha Boarding Secondary School (SBSS) premises located in Jhapa, Nepal.

Nidi Secondary School & Indreni Campus
High school in Ram Chowk Birtamod

Address: Jhapa, Nepal
Phone: 9801268585
Email: info@whitehouseeducation.edu.np

entries.question: Why should I choose WCBT?
entries.answer:
Students choose WCBT because of:
• Kathmandu University-affiliated programs
• Technology-focused education
• Industry-oriented curriculum
• Modern learning environment
• Computer laboratories
• Digital learning systems
• Career counseling support
• Internship and industry exposure
• Research and innovation culture
• Affordable access to quality education in Eastern Nepal

entries.question: What programs are offered?
entries.answer:
Current Programs:
• Bachelor in Information Technology (BIT)
• Bachelor of Technology in Educational Information Technology (B.Tech Ed IT)

Future Programs may include:
• BHI
• BIM
• BBA
• MBA
• AI and Data Science Programs
• Professional Certifications

entries.question: What is BIT?
entries.answer:
Bachelor in Information Technology (BIT) is a technology-focused undergraduate program designed to develop expertise in:
• Programming
• Software Development
• Database Systems
• Networking
• Cybersecurity
• Cloud Computing
• Artificial Intelligence
• Web and Mobile Development

entries.question: What is B.Tech Ed IT?
entries.answer:
Bachelor of Technology in Educational Information Technology (B.Tech Ed IT) combines technology and education.

The program prepares graduates for careers in:
• Educational Technology
• Digital Learning Design
• E-Learning Systems
• Instructional Design
• Learning Management Systems
• Educational Research
• ICT in Education

entries.question: Who can apply for BIT?
entries.answer:
Students who have completed Grade XII or equivalent qualifications recognized by Kathmandu University and meet KU eligibility requirements may apply for BIT.

For detailed requirements, please contact admissions or refer to Kathmandu University's official eligibility criteria.

entries.question: Who can apply for B.Tech Ed IT?
entries.answer:
Students who have completed Grade XII or equivalent qualifications recognized by Kathmandu University may apply for B.Tech Ed IT, subject to program eligibility requirements.

For official eligibility details, please contact the admissions office.

entries.question: How can I apply?
entries.answer:
The admission process generally includes:
1. Application Submission
2. Entrance Examination
3. Interview (if applicable)
4. Admission Confirmation
5. Fee Payment
6. Enrollment

Contact us at info@whitehouseeducation.edu.np or 9801268585 for the latest admission guidance.

entries.question: Is there an entrance examination?
entries.answer:
Yes. Admissions are conducted according to Kathmandu University admission procedures and requirements.

Specific details are announced during the admission cycle. Please contact admissions for the latest information.

entries.question: When will admissions open?
entries.answer:
Admissions generally open before each academic intake.

For the latest updates, contact:
Phone: 9801268585
Mobile: 9801268585
Email: info@whitehouseeducation.edu.np
Website: whitehouseeducation.edu.np

entries.question: What is the fee structure for BIT?
entries.answer:
BIT fees follow Kathmandu University's approved program structure, with additional college-level charges possible.

For the latest total fee schedule and any WCBT-specific charges, please confirm during admission.

entries.question: What is the fee structure for B.Tech Ed IT?
entries.answer:
B.Tech Ed IT fees follow Kathmandu University's approved program framework, while WCBT may add college-level charges.

Please check the latest fee schedule during admission for the most accurate total cost.

entries.question: Are scholarships available?
entries.answer:
Yes. Scholarship schemes may include:
• Merit Scholarships
• Need-Based Scholarships
• Institutional Scholarships
• Special Category Scholarships
• Partner Organization Scholarships

Details are announced annually. Contact admissions for current scholarship information.

entries.question: What facilities are available?
entries.answer:
Students will have access to:
• Modern Classrooms
• Computer Labs
• Library
• Digital Resources
• Conference Hall
• Cafeteria
• Sports Facilities
• Counseling Services
• Student Clubs
• Parking Facilities
• Internet Connectivity

entries.question: Is there a library?
entries.answer:
Yes. The library includes:
• Physical Books
• Reference Materials
• Journals
• Digital Resources
• E-books
• Research Materials

entries.question: Are computer labs available?
entries.answer: Yes. Dedicated computer laboratories are being developed to support both BIT and B.Tech Ed IT programs.

entries.question: Will students receive internship opportunities?
entries.answer: WCBT aims to establish partnerships with industry organizations to facilitate internships, projects, and workplace learning opportunities.

entries.question: What career opportunities are available after BIT?
entries.answer:
Graduates may work as:
• Software Developer
• System Analyst
• Network Administrator
• Cloud Engineer
• AI Developer
• Cybersecurity Specialist
• Database Administrator
• IT Consultant
• Entrepreneur

entries.question: What career opportunities are available after B.Tech Ed IT?
entries.answer:
Graduates may work as:
• Educational Technologist
• LMS Administrator
• Instructional Designer
• E-Learning Specialist
• ICT Trainer
• Educational Researcher
• Academic Technology Coordinator

entries.question: Are there clubs and student activities?
entries.answer:
Yes. Planned student engagement includes:
• IT Club
• Robotics Club
• Innovation Club
• Research Club
• Sports Activities
• Cultural Programs
• Workshops
• Hackathons
• Community Service Projects

entries.question: Does WCBT organize hackathons?
entries.answer: Yes. Hackathons, coding competitions, innovation challenges, workshops, and technology events are planned as part of student development activities.

entries.question: Is the campus safe?
entries.answer:
Student safety is a priority. The institution is implementing:
• Fire Safety Measures
• Emergency Exit Plans
• Structural Safety Compliance
• Drinking Water Safety Measures
• Sanitation and Hygiene Standards
• First Aid Facilities

entries.question: How can I contact WCBT?
entries.answer:
You can contact WCBT at:
Phone: 9801268585
Mobile: 9801268585
Email: info@whitehouseeducation.edu.np
Website: whitehouseeducation.edu.np
Location: Jhapa, Nepal

entries.question: Why should parents trust WCBT?
entries.answer:
Parents trust WCBT because it combines:
• Educational Legacy
• Industry-Relevant Programs
• Technology-Driven Learning
• Student Support Services
• Career-Oriented Education
• Academic Quality Standards
• Focus on Employability

entries.question: How does WCBT support student success?
entries.answer:
The college provides:
• Academic Mentoring
• Counseling Services
• Workshops and Seminars
• Career Guidance
• Internship Support
• Industry Exposure
• Project-Based Learning

entries.question: What are the tuition and fees?
entries.answer:
Program fees are based on Kathmandu University's approved structure, plus any additional WCBT college charges.

Fees are subject to approval, periodic revision, and final confirmation at the time of admission. Contact admissions for the latest details.

entries.question: What career opportunities are available?
entries.answer:
After BIT, graduates may work as Software Developer, System Analyst, Network Administrator, Cloud Engineer, AI Developer, Cybersecurity Specialist, and more.

After B.Tech Ed IT, graduates may work as Educational Technologist, LMS Administrator, Instructional Designer, E-Learning Specialist, ICT Trainer, and more.

Ask about BIT or B.Tech Ed IT for a detailed career list.

entries.question: Is WCBT affiliated with Kathmandu University?
entries.answer: Yes. WCBT offers Kathmandu University-affiliated programs including BIT and B.Tech Ed IT, designed to meet KU academic standards with a technology-focused, industry-oriented approach.

entries.question: Is hostel accommodation available?
entries.answer: Hostel and accommodation details are shared during the admission process. For the latest availability and facilities, please contact admissions at 9801268585 or info@whitehouseeducation.edu.np.

entries.question: What are the office hours?
entries.answer:
For current office hours and visit timings, please contact:
Phone: 9801268585
Email: info@whitehouseeducation.edu.np

Our team will share the latest schedule.

entries.question: How long is the program?
entries.answer:
Both BIT and B.Tech Ed IT are four-year undergraduate programs under Kathmandu University affiliation.

For semester-wise structure details, contact the admissions office.

entries.question: What documents are required for admission?
entries.answer:
Admission documents typically include academic certificates, transcripts/marksheets, identification documents, and other materials as per Kathmandu University and WCBT admission guidelines.

Contact admissions for the complete checklist for the current intake.

entries.question: Can I visit the campus?
entries.answer:
Yes! Prospective students and parents are welcome to visit WCBT in Jhapa.

Please contact us in advance to schedule a campus visit:
Phone: 9801268585
Email: info@whitehouseeducation.edu.np

entries.question: What is the eligibility for admission?
entries.answer:
Generally, students who have completed Grade XII (Plus 2) or equivalent qualifications recognized by Kathmandu University may apply for BIT and B.Tech Ed IT, subject to program-specific requirements.

Contact admissions for the latest eligibility criteria.

entries.question: Does WCBT offer online or distance learning?
entries.answer: WCBT focuses on technology-driven, campus-based learning with modern digital tools and resources. For current learning mode details, please contact the admissions office.

entries.question: Is there a cafeteria or food facility?
entries.answer: Yes. Cafeteria facilities are part of the planned campus amenities for students at WCBT.

entries.question: Are sports facilities available?
entries.answer: Yes. Sports facilities are available as part of student life and campus amenities at WCBT, alongside clubs, cultural programs, and other activities.

entries.question: How is WCBT different from other colleges?
entries.answer:
WCBT stands out with:
• Kathmandu University affiliation
• Strong focus on IT & educational technology
• Industry-oriented curriculum
• Innovation & research culture
• Career support & internship focus
• Quality education access in Eastern Nepal
